item_a.name: Bose Noise Cancelling Headphones 700
item_a.image_alt: Bose Noise Cancelling Headphones 700 in black with sleek modern design
pros: Intuitive touch controls and physical buttons
Comfortable for extended wear with premium materials
Excellent companion app with customizable EQ
cons: Higher price point without significant advantages
Cannot fold flat for compact storage
item_a.best_for: Professionals who prioritize crystal-clear call quality and premium build over maximum battery life.

item_b.name: Sony WH-1000XM5
item_b.image_alt: Sony WH-1000XM5 wireless noise cancelling headphones with sleek minimalist design
pros: Superior ANC with 8 microphones and dual processors
Exceptional sound quality with LDAC codec support
Advanced features like Speak-to-Chat and multipoint connectivity
cons: Non-folding design takes up more space
Touch controls can be overly sensitive
item_b.best_for: Audiophiles and frequent travelers seeking the best noise cancellation and longest battery life available.

comparison_summary: The Sony WH-1000XM5 and Bose 700 both cost about the same but serve different masters. Sony wins on noise cancellation, battery life (30 vs 20 hours), and audio quality. Bose answers back with better call quality and more controllable ANC levels.
verdict_text: Sony WH-1000XM5 takes the win with better noise cancellation, 50% longer battery, and richer sound—unless you live on video calls, where Bose 700 dominates.

key_differences.item_b: Very good with AI-based noise reduction

seo_content:
I've spent weeks testing both the Bose 700 and Sony WH-1000XM5, and honestly, choosing between these flagship noise-cancelling headphones comes down to what matters most in your daily life.

The Sony WH-1000XM5 has rightfully earned its reputation as the ANC king. Those dual QN1 processors paired with an 8-microphone array deliver what I consider the best noise cancellation you can buy right now. The 30-hour battery life absolutely crushes the Bose 700's 20 hours—that's 50% more listening time, which makes a real difference on long flights or when you forget to charge overnight. Sony also throws in LDAC codec support for hi-res audio, something audiophiles will appreciate.

Bose fights back where it counts for professionals: call quality. That adaptive 8-microphone system with beamforming technology makes the 700 unbeatable for video meetings. I've taken calls in noisy coffee shops, and people on the other end couldn't tell I wasn't in a quiet room. The physical buttons alongside touch controls are also refreshing—no more struggling with frozen touch surfaces in winter. The Bose Music app gives you 11 ANC levels compared to Sony's more basic adjustments.

Sound-wise, Sony delivers warmer, bass-heavy audio that's perfect for pop and electronic music. Bose goes neutral and balanced, which classical listeners might prefer. Both let you tweak EQ settings extensively.

Build quality is excellent on both. Neither folds compact anymore, which is a bummer. The WH-1000XM5 looks sleeker with softer materials, while the Bose 700's stainless steel headband feels more premium to me. Both weigh exactly 250g and stay comfortable for hours.

Pricing sits between $329-$429 for both. For most people, Sony offers better value with that battery life and superior ANC. But if you're constantly on calls? The Bose 700 deserves serious consideration.

faq.q: Which has better noise cancellation, Bose 700 or Sony WH-1000XM5?
faq.a: Sony WH-1000XM5 wins here. Its dual-processor system blocks 2-3 dB more ambient noise across most frequencies, especially in the mid-range where voices live. You'll notice the difference on planes and in busy offices. The Bose 700 is still good, just not quite as effective.

faq.q: Can the Bose 700 or Sony WH-1000XM5 fold for travel?
faq.a: Nope, neither one folds at the hinges anymore. They only fold flat at the ear cups to fit in their cases. This makes them bulkier than older models like the Sony WH-1000XM4 or Bose QuietComfort series. Kind of annoying if you're tight on bag space.

faq.q: Which headphones are better for phone calls and video meetings?
faq.a: Bose 700 hands down. That adaptive 8-microphone system isolates your voice way better in noisy places. The Sony WH-1000XM5 has decent AI-based noise reduction for calls, but the Bose consistently sounds clearer and more natural according to pretty much everyone who's tested both.

faq.q: Does the Sony WH-1000XM5 sound better than the Bose 700?
faq.a: Most people will say yes. Sony gives you richer bass, more detailed highs, and LDAC support for hi-res audio—things the Bose 700 just doesn't have. Bose sounds more neutral and balanced. Both have great EQ customization in their apps, so your personal taste matters a lot here.

faq.q: How much longer does the Sony WH-1000XM5 battery last compared to Bose 700?
faq.a: Sony gives you 30 hours with ANC on versus Bose's 20 hours—that's a huge 50% more playback time. Quick charging is similar: Sony gets you 3 hours from 3 minutes of charging, Bose gives 3.5 hours from 15 minutes. If you travel a lot or hate charging constantly, Sony's advantage really adds up.

faq.q: Is Bose Noise Cancelling Headphones 700 better than Sony WH-1000XM5?
faq.a: Not overall, but it depends on your priorities. The Sony WH-1000XM5 edges out the Bose 700 with superior noise cancellation, 50% longer battery life (30 vs 20 hours), and richer sound quality. However, if you spend most of your time on video calls, the Bose 700's exceptional call quality makes it the better choice for you.

faq.q: Should I buy Bose Noise Cancelling Headphones 700 or Sony WH-1000XM5?
faq.a: Choose Sony WH-1000XM5 if you prioritize noise cancellation, battery endurance, and audio quality for music and media. Pick Bose 700 if call quality is your main concern or you want more granular control over noise cancellation levels. Both are priced similarly, so your use case should drive the decision.

faq.q: What is the difference between Bose Noise Cancelling Headphones 700 and Sony WH-1000XM5?
faq.a: The Sony WH-1000XM5 delivers superior noise cancellation and richer audio, plus 30 hours of battery versus Bose's 20 hours. The Bose 700 counters with best-in-class call quality and more adjustable ANC settings, making it ideal for frequent video conferencing. Both cost roughly the same, making this a choice between Sony's audio excellence and Bose's communication prowess.
